ucs.title: [LLM] APT12 (Numbered Panda) DNSCALC C2 — port derived from DNS A-record octets
ucs.description: Hunts APT12's signature DNSCALC tradecraft where the backdoor (e.g., variants of Etumbot/IXESHE) resolves a benign-looking domain, then uses the last octets of the returned A record to compute a non-standard high TCP port for C2. Detection chains a DNS resolution event with an immediate outbound connect from the same process to that IP on an unusual >1024 port.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as first_seen from datamodel=Network_Resolution.DNS where DNS.message_type="RESPONSE" AND DNS.answer!="unknown" by DNS.src DNS.query DNS.answer _time span=1m | rename DNS.src as src DNS.query as query DNS.answer as resolved_ip | eval octets=split(resolved_ip,".") | eval o2=tonumber(mvindex(octets,1)), o3=tonumber(mvindex(octets,2)), o4=tonumber(mvindex(octets,3)) | eval calc_port_a=(o3*256)+o4, calc_port_b=(o2*256)+o3, calc_port_c=o2+o3+o4 | join type=inner src resolved_ip [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port>1024 AND All_Traffic.dest_port NOT IN (3389,5985,5986,8080,8443,9090,1433,1521) AND All_Traffic.action="allowed" by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port All_Traffic.app _time span=2m | rename All_Traffic.src as src All_Traffic.dest as resolved_ip All_Traffic.dest_port as dest_port All_Traffic.app as process] | where dest_port=calc_port_a OR dest_port=calc_port_b OR dest_port=calc_port_c | stats count values(query) as queried_domain values(process) as process by src resolved_ip dest_port | where count>=1
ucs.kql: let dnsResolutions = DeviceEvents | where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse" | extend parsed = parse_json(AdditionalFields) | extend QueryName = tostring(parsed.QueryName), ResolvedIP = tostring(parsed.IPAddresses) | where isnotempty(ResolvedIP) and ResolvedIP matches regex @"^\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+$" | extend o = split(ResolvedIP, ".") | extend o2 = toint(o[1]), o3 = toint(o[2]), o4 = toint(o[3]) | extend calc_a = (o3*256)+o4, calc_b = (o2*256)+o3, calc_c = o2+o3+o4 | project DnsTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, QueryName, ResolvedIP, calc_a, calc_b, calc_c; let outbound = DeviceNetworkEvents | where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess" | where RemotePort > 1024 and RemotePort !in (3389, 5985, 5986, 8080, 8443, 9090, 1433, 1521, 8000, 8888) | where RemoteIPType == "Public"; outbound | join kind=inner dnsResolutions on DeviceId | where RemoteIP == ResolvedIP | where (DnsTime between (Timestamp - 5m .. Timestamp + 30s)) | where RemotePort == calc_a or RemotePort == calc_b or RemotePort == calc_c | project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, QueryName, RemoteIP, RemotePort, calc_a, calc_b, calc_c, InitiatingProcessAccountName | summarize Hits=count(), Domains=make_set(QueryName, 5), Processes=make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 5) by DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort
ucs.rationale: DNSCALC is a near-unique APT12/Numbered Panda algorithm documented by FireEye (Etumbot/IXESHE families) — using DNS A-record octets to derive C2 port. Most APTs hard-code ports or use 80/443; matching resolved-IP→connect-port arithmetic on the same host within seconds is rare outside this actor's toolset and produces very few legitimate matches.

ucs.title: [LLM] APT12 IXESHE delivery chain — weaponized Office doc → CVE-2012-0158/CVE-2014-1761 child process → web-service C2 beacon
ucs.description: Hunts APT12's characteristic spearphishing-attachment intrusion chain against media/government/high-tech verticals: a malicious .doc/.xls/.rtf opened from Outlook spawns an unusual child (rundll32, regsvr32, or a dropped binary in %TEMP%/%APPDATA%) that subsequently beacons to a free blog/forum/web-mail service used for IXESHE bidirectional C2.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as proc_time values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as proc_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("WINWORD.EXE","EXCEL.EXE","POWERPNT.EXE","OUTLOOK.EXE") AND (Processes.process_name IN ("rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe") OR match(Processes.process_path,"(?i)\\\\(Temp|AppData\\\\Local|AppData\\\\Roaming|ProgramData)\\\\")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_id _time span=5m | rename Processes.* as * | join type=inner dest process_id [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_category!="internal" by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.process_id _time span=10m | rename All_Traffic.src as dest All_Traffic.process_id as process_id All_Traffic.dest as c2_dest All_Traffic.dest_port as c2_port] | lookup web_categories domain as c2_dest OUTPUT category | search category IN ("blogs","forums","webmail","dynamic-dns","newly-registered","content-hosting") OR c2_dest IN ("*.blogspot.com","*.wordpress.com","*.appspot.com","*.tumblr.com") | stats count values(parent_process_name) as parent values(process_name) as child values(cmdline) as cmd values(c2_dest) as c2 values(c2_port) as c2_ports by dest user
ucs.kql: let suspChildren = dynamic(["rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe"]); let officeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe"]); let webServiceC2 = dynamic(["blogspot.com","wordpress.com","appspot.com","tumblr.com","livejournal.com","github.io","pages.dev","workers.dev","weebly.com","blogger.com"]); let officeChild = DeviceProcessEvents | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeParents) | where FileName in~ (suspChildren) or FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\") | project ChildTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, ChildPid=ProcessId, ChildName=FileName, ChildCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ParentName=InitiatingProcessFileName, AccountName; let netFromChild = DeviceNetworkEvents | where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess" and RemoteIPType == "Public" | where RemoteUrl has_any (webServiceC2) or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (suspChildren) | project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, NetPid=InitiatingProcessId, NetProc=InitiatingProcessFileName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort; officeChild | join kind=inner netFromChild on DeviceId | where NetTime between (ChildTime .. ChildTime + 30m) | where ChildPid == NetPid or NetProc =~ ChildName | join kind=leftouter (EmailAttachmentInfo | where FileType in~ ("doc","docx","xls","xlsx","rtf","ppt","pptx","hwp") | project AttachmentTime=Timestamp, RecipientEmailAddress, FileName, SenderFromAddress, SubjectLine=tostring(parse_json(tostring(NetworkMessageId)))) on $left.AccountName == $right.RecipientEmailAddress | project ChildTime, DeviceName, AccountName, ParentName, ChildName, ChildCmd, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort, SenderFromAddress, FileName | summarize Events=count() by DeviceName, AccountName, ParentName, ChildName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
ucs.rationale: APT12/IXESHE/Etumbot has consistently chained spearphishing attachments exploiting Office CVEs (CVE-2012-0158, CVE-2014-1761) with bidirectional C2 hidden in legitimate web services (blogs, forums) — documented by Trend Micro and FireEye. The Office-parent → LOLBin/temp-binary → blogspot/wordpress C2 pattern is highly specific to Numbered Panda's media/government targeting and rarely appears in be
